data.Name: Frag grenade I
data.Description: A fragmentary, or frag, grenade explodes in a cloud of shrapnel.
data.Properties_Description: Explode: Explosives have the explode special property, which lists the amount of damage the explosion deals and the damage type (if any), special effects (with a duration, if necessary), and the radius of the explosion. When you attack with this type of weapon or ammunition, aim at a grid intersection. Each creature within the blast radius takes the listed damage but can attempt a Reflex saving throw for half damage. If you score a critical hit, it applies only to the creature closest to the targeted intersection (you choose the creature if several are equally close). If the explode special property has any special effects other than damage, they are negated with a successful saving throw.

data.Name: Shock grenade I
data.Description: A shock grenade releases a pulse of electrical energy on impact.
data.Properties_Description: Explode: Explosives have the explode special property, which lists the amount of damage the explosion deals and the damage type (if any), special effects (with a duration, if necessary), and the radius of the explosion. When you attack with this type of weapon or ammunition, aim at a grid intersection. Each creature within the blast radius takes the listed damage but can attempt a Reflex saving throw for half damage. If you score a critical hit, it applies only to the creature closest to the targeted intersection (you choose the creature if several are equally close). If the explode special property has any special effects other than damage, they are negated with a successful saving throw.

data.Name: Smoke grenade
data.Description: A smoke grenade deals no damage; instead, it releases a cloud of dense smoke. Each character who inhales smoke must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw each round (DC = 15 + 1 per previous check) or spend that round choking and coughing; he can do nothing else. A character who chokes for 2 consecutive rounds takes 1d6 nonlethal damage. (Active environmental protection from a suit of armor prevents this effect altogether.) Regardless of the armor a character wears, smoke obscures vision, providing concealment.

data.Name: Holy water grenade I
data.Description: Produced by priests of good-aligned deities, holy water grenades explode with a spray of consecrated water that harms undead creatures and evil outsiders while leaving other creatures and objects merely damp. This typically isn't enough water to damage computers or other electronic or technological items.
data.Properties_Description: Explode: Explosives have the explode special property, which lists the amount of damage the explosion deals and the damage type (if any), special effects (with a duration, if necessary), and the radius of the explosion. data.Name: Riot grenade I
data.Description: Riot grenades are standard issue among police forces, as they deal nonlethal damage and slow the movements of creatures affected by the blast.

If the explode special property has any special effects other than damage, they are negated with a successful saving throw.

data.Name: Foam grenade I
data.Description: A foam grenade explodes on impact to release a burst of fire-suppressant foam akin to that from a fire extinguisher. This foam reduces the damage from the burning condition or from the corrode critical hit effect taken by creatures within the area by the listed amount each round; this ends the burning condition or corrode effect if it reduces the amount of damage to 0. This also ends any active fires within the affected area.
data.Properties_Description: Explode: Explosives have the explode special property, which lists the amount of damage the explosion deals and the damage type (if any), special effects (with a duration, if necessary), and the radius of the explosion. If you score a critical hit, it applies only to the creature closest to the targeted intersection (you choose the creature if several are equally close). If the explode special property has any special effects other than damage, they are negated with a successful saving throw.

data.Name: Web grenade I
data.Description: Developed using samples of haans' biological webbing, a web grenade explodes to cover the area in sticky strands of hightensile fibers, making the affected area difficult terrain.
data.Properties_Description: Explode: Explosives have the explode special property, which lists the amount of damage the explosion deals and the damage type (if any), special effects (with a duration, if necessary), and the radius of the explosion. data.Name: Pulse grenade I
data.Description: A pulse grenade emits a powerful EMP blast on impact whose damage and effects apply only to constructs with the technological subtype. Constructs reduced to 0 HP by a pulse grenade are not destroyed but simply nonfunctional until restored to 1 or more Hit Points. The EMP wave from a pulse grenade is not intense enough to affect augmentations, weapons, armor, or other technological gear.

If you score a critical hit, it applies only to the creature closest to the targeted intersection (you choose the creature if several are equally close). If the explode special property has any special effects other than damage, they are negated with a successful saving throw.

data.Name: Riot grenade III
data.Description: Riot grenades are standard issue among police forces, as they deal nonlethal damage and slow the movements of creatures affected by the blast.
data.Properties_Description: Explode: Explosives have the explode special property, which lists the amount of damage the explosion deals and the damage type (if any), special effects (with a duration, if necessary), and the radius of the explosion. data.Name: Foam grenade II
data.Description: A foam grenade explodes on impact to release a burst of fire-suppressant foam akin to that from a fire extinguisher. This foam reduces the damage from the burning condition or from the corrode critical hit effect taken by creatures within the area by the listed amount each round; this ends the burning condition or corrode effect if it reduces the amount of damage to 0. This also ends any active fires within the affected area.

data.Name: Pulse grenade II
data.Description: A pulse grenade emits a powerful EMP blast on impact whose damage and effects apply only to constructs with the technological subtype. Constructs reduced to 0 HP by a pulse grenade are not destroyed but simply nonfunctional until restored to 1 or more Hit Points. The EMP wave from a pulse grenade is not intense enough to affect augmentations, weapons, armor, or other technological gear.
